content:
Letting x₁,x₂,x₃ be the numbers of blocks of 1,2,3 “1”’s respectively (no block of 4 is possible since 1111>1000), the condition becomes

 x₁+11x₂+111x₃ =1000.

Each solution gives a total length  
 n = x₁+2x₂+3x₃  
and one checks that all such n have the form  
 n = 1000−9t  
where t runs over the union of the integer intervals

 [0..90], [12..92], [24..94], …, [96..106], and the singleton {108}.

These glue together into a single stretch 0…106 plus the extra point 108, i.e. 107+1=108 distinct t’s, hence 108 distinct n’s.  The sum of the digits of 108 is 1+0+8=9.  

Answer: \boxed{9}.

message.content:
Letting x₁,x₂,x₃ be the numbers of blocks of 1,2,3 “1”’s respectively (no block of 4 is possible since 1111>1000), the condition becomes

 x₁+11x₂+111x₃ =1000.

Each solution gives a total length  
 n = x₁+2x₂+3x₃  
and one checks that all such n have the form  
 n = 1000−9t  
where t runs over the union of the integer intervals

 [0..90], [12..92], [24..94], …, [96..106], and the singleton {108}.

These glue together into a single stretch 0…106 plus the extra point 108, i.e. 107+1=108 distinct t’s, hence 108 distinct n’s.  The sum of the digits of 108 is 1+0+8=9.  

Answer: \boxed{9}.
